response.justification: The candidate is a capable generalist full-stack engineer with solid software craftsmanship fundamentals and breadth across languages, but lacks virtually all of the specialized depth this role demands. There is no evidence of experimentation platform work, A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics pipelines, statistical analysis, or growth engineering. The role is IC6 Principal requiring enterprise-scale cross-team ownership of experimentation systems; the candidate's background reflects a versatile individual contributor who builds products, not measurement or rollout infrastructure. The gap between the candidate's profile and the role's core requirements is too wide to bridge at this seniority level.
strengths: Long career spanning 15+ years with exposure to multiple languages (C, C++, Java, TypeScript, C#, DLang) satisfying the language breadth requirement
Has handled high-throughput distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at one role) and current AI-agent/MCP tooling work shows adaptability to modern paradigms
Consistent software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and community knowledge-sharing (workshops, open-source) align with mentoring expectations
concerns: Zero experimentation platform experience — no A/B testing, feature flagging, rollout risk management, bias detection, or experiment assignment infrastructure mentioned anywhere
No telemetry, metrics, or observability infrastructure work; data aptitude is undemonstrated and Python (common in data/analysis work) is absent from the skills section
Current and past titles are Staff/Senior, not Principal; no evidence of leading multi-team initiatives, cross-org influence, or architectural ownership at the IC6 scope this role requires

key_factors.factor: Missing telemetry and data aptitude evidence
